Write a Python function `is_palindrome(s: str) -> bool` in a file named palindrome.py. It must ignore case, spaces, and non-alphanumeric characters (so "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama" returns True). Below the function, add at least 3 assert-based tests covering: a mixed-case punctuated palindrome (True), a non-palindrome (False), and the empty string (True). Commit the file to git.

This repo is mobee. BUG in the network observatory page (web/network/): the seller census only ever shows ONE seller even when many are live. ROOT CAUSE (verified): web/network/js/store.js line ~85 keys NIP-89 handler announcements by `normalized.handler?.d || normalized.id` — but EVERY mobee seller publishes kind-31990 with the SAME d-tag "mobee-seller", so all sellers collapse into one map entry (newest wins). Addressable nostr events are keyed by (kind, pubkey, d-tag) — the author must be part of the key. FIX: key handlers by pubkey+d (e.g. `${ev.pubkey}:${d}`), keep the newest-per-key replacement logic, and make sure every view that consumes handlers (views.js census/seller list) renders one row PER SELLER pubkey. Apply the same fix to web/network/dist/ copies if they are checked-in build artifacts (mirror the change so the deployed page gets it). Verify by reading the consuming views for other spots assuming a single handler. Deliver as ONE git commit on a fork branch descending from the pinned base.
git commit on a fork branch

Rust change in crates/mobee/src/agent_presets.rs (+ small plumbing where noted). Goal: agent presets must be extensible and free of machine-specific paths.

1. REMOVE both hardcoded '/srv/forge/tools/codex-acp-ng/node_modules/.bin/codex-acp' references (detect_available_agents and resolve_codex). codex resolution = which('codex-acp') then npx fallback, nothing else.
2. ADD config-defined presets: seller config gains an optional [agents] table mapping preset name -> argv array (e.g. [agents.grok] argv = ['grok','agent','stdio']). Find the seller config struct (crates/mobee-core/src/home.rs / config.toml parsing) and add the field. resolve_agent_preset and detect_available_agents take the custom table into account: custom names resolve to their argv; a custom entry with a built-in name OVERRIDES the built-in; detect includes a custom preset when argv[0] resolves on PATH or is an existing file path. Keep resolve/detect signatures workable — thread the config through the existing call sites in crates/mobee/src/sell.rs and main.rs (there are few).
3. Harness label: when a custom preset is used, the harness label reported in results = the preset name (find where the built-in labels claude/cursor/codex are attached and use the same seam).
4. Unknown preset error message mentions both built-ins and any configured names.
5. Tests in agent_presets.rs style: custom preset resolves; custom overrides built-in; unknown still errors; no test depends on machine paths. Run: cargo test -p mobee --all-features (must be green) and grep -rn 'srv/forge' crates/mobee/ must return NOTHING.
6. Do NOT touch payment/money files (seller.rs, payment*.rs, authorize_pay.rs, receipt.rs, payment_wallet.rs) — if you think you need to, stop and deliver a written explanation instead.

Deliver: single commit on a fork branch as usual. Commit message: 'feat(seller): config-defined agent presets; drop machine-specific paths'.
git commit on a fork branch

PIECE-14 Job A: offer schema drops mint_url + protocol version bump. FIRST read the design doc in the repo: docs/meta/PIECE-14-PAYMENT-FLOW.md — implement exactly its §'Job A' scope, no more.

Scope (crates/mobee-core/src/gateway.rs ONLY, plus its own tests):
1. Remove OfferDraft.mint_url (gateway.rs:55) and the emitted mint tag (gateway.rs:101).
2. Remove ParsedOffer.mint_url (gateway.rs:120) and the mint-tag read in parse_offer (gateway.rs:305-307); a mint tag on an incoming offer is ignored, not required.
3. Bump PROTOCOL_VERSION "1" -> "2" (gateway.rs:8).
4. Fix compile fallout ONLY where gateway.rs consumers reference the removed field — if that fallout reaches into seller.rs/seller_daemon.rs/payment files beyond deleting a field access or a fixture line, STOP and deliver a written explanation instead (those are jobs B-E).

Acceptance (run and make green):
- grep -n 'mint_url' crates/mobee-core/src/gateway.rs -> no matches
- grep -n 'PROTOCOL_VERSION' crates/mobee-core/src/gateway.rs -> shows "2"
- cargo test -p mobee-core --all-features -> green (fix/adjust existing tests that asserted the mint tag or v=1; add: a v2 offer round-trips without a mint tag; a v=1 event fails parse_offer with UnsupportedVersion)
- cargo build --workspace -> green

Commit message: 'feat(gateway)!: drop offer mint_url, bump protocol version to 2 (PIECE-14 A)'. Single commit on a fork branch as usual.
git commit on a fork branch

Rust fix in crates/mobee-core/src/seller_daemon.rs: agent-run failures publish a kind-7000 status=error event with EMPTY content — the buyer gets no reason at all (field evidence today: a seller errored 4 min into a job and the error event content was empty). Rate-gate refusals already carry a reason in content via error_draft(offer_id, buyer_pubkey, seller_pubkey, content) — agent-run/delivery failures must do the same.

Scope:
1. Find every seller_daemon code path that publishes a 7000 status=error with empty or missing content (agent spawn failure, agent run failure/timeout, fork/push failure, any other job-abort path). Enumerate them in your commit message body.
2. Plumb a reason string into each: 'reason_code: human message' format, mirroring the existing rate-gate style. reason_code from a small closed set you define (e.g. agent_spawn_failed, agent_run_failed, agent_timeout, git_fork_failed, git_push_failed, internal). Truncate the human part to <=300 chars and strip absolute filesystem paths from it (no leaking seller machine layout).
3. Tests: one per plumbed path where feasible — assert the published error event content is non-empty and starts with the reason_code. Follow existing seller_daemon test patterns.
4. Do NOT touch payment/money files (seller.rs, payment*.rs, authorize_pay.rs, receipt.rs, payment_wallet.rs). Do not restructure the daemon — reason plumbing only.

Acceptance (run, make green):
- cargo test -p mobee-core --all-features -> green EXCEPT exactly these 3 known live-mint failures (mint is down): setup_wallet_funds_testnut_and_never_emits_secret_key, live_testnut_fund_observes_balance_gt_zero, authorize_pay_refuses_ext_locator_via_pay_path_verifier
- grep your new reason codes in seller_daemon.rs tests -> each abort path covered or explicitly noted why not testable

Commit message: 'fix(seller): agent-run and delivery failures publish machine-readable error reasons'. Single commit on a fork branch as usual.
git commit on a fork branch

New Rust bin crate crates/mobee-collector: a market observability collector for the mobee relay. ENVIRONMENT NOTE: if cargo is not on PATH, run: source /srv/forge/servers/default/agents/keeper:mobee-orchestrator/tools/mobee-e2e-toolkit/mobee-buildenv.sh

Scope:
1. New workspace member crates/mobee-collector (add to root Cargo.toml members). Dependencies: reuse what the workspace already has (nostr-sdk, tokio, serde) + rusqlite (bundled feature).
2. Subcommand 'run': connect to a relay (--relay, default wss://mobee-relay.orveth.dev; handle NIP-42 AUTH with a key from --keyfile or NOSTR_PRIVATE_KEY env), subscribe to kinds 5109, 7000, 6109, 3400, 31990, and persist every event into sqlite (--db path, default ./mobee-collector.db): table events(id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, kind INT, pubkey TEXT, created_at INT, tags_json TEXT, content TEXT, raw_json TEXT). INSERT OR IGNORE (idempotent re-runs). Also backfill: on start, REQ historical events (no since filter) before streaming live.
3. Subcommand 'report': read the db, print a funnel summary: offers total (by targeted vs pool), claim rate (offers with >=1 kind-7000 status=processing), delivery rate (>=1 6109), receipt rate (>=1 3400), median time-to-claim and time-to-deliver (created_at deltas), breakdown of 7000 status=error counts by whether content is empty, per-seller table (pubkey short, claims, deliveries, receipts), per-harness table (harness tag on 6109). Plain text output, no fancy formatting.
4. Tests: unit tests over fixture events (construct events in-test, insert, assert report numbers). No live-relay tests.
5. Do NOT touch any existing crate code except the workspace members list. Do NOT touch payment/money files at all.

Acceptance (run and make green):
- cargo build -p mobee-collector -> green
- cargo test -p mobee-collector -> green
- cargo test --workspace --all-features -> no NEW failures vs base (3 known live-mint failures allowed: setup_wallet_funds_testnut_and_never_emits_secret_key, live_testnut_fund_observes_balance_gt_zero, authorize_pay_refuses_ext_locator_via_pay_path_verifier)

Commit message: 'feat(collector): relay event collector + funnel report (observability layer 1)'. Single commit on a fork branch as usual.
git commit on a fork branch

PIECE-14 v2 build, Job B: seller accepted_mints config + gate relaxation. FIRST read docs/meta/PIECE-14-PAYMENT-FLOW.md in the repo — implement exactly §'Job B' (its scope paragraph + acceptance block are the contract). Summary of that scope:

1. Replace top-level MobeeConfig.mint_url with accepted_mints: Vec<String> (default [DEFAULT_MINT_URL]); legacy mint_url in an existing config.toml migrates to accepted_mints=[that value] on load (shim near the existing migrate_dead_mint_url).
2. Boot gate: 'must equal DEFAULT_MINT_URL' becomes 'every accepted_mints entry is allow-listed' (testnut/dev fence stays).
3. Delete the offer mint gate (seller_daemon.rs ~:522) and retire OfferSkip::NonTestnutMint.
4. Re-source episode.mint (journal) and TEMPORARILY build the redeem PaymentPolicy (~:812/:826) from accepted_mints. ParsedOffer.mint_url STAYS present and readable — later jobs re-point those reads.
5. seller.rs fixture rides along (fixture-line edits only there).

CLARIFICATION not yet in the doc: the config ALSO has extra_mints (buyer-wallet-level allowed mints, used by wallet mints add). accepted_mints is a NEW SEPARATE field (seller payment policy) — do NOT merge or repurpose extra_mints; if the distinction needs a doc comment, write it at the field definition.

ENVIRONMENT: if cargo missing: source /srv/forge/servers/default/agents/keeper:mobee-orchestrator/tools/mobee-e2e-toolkit/mobee-buildenv.sh

Acceptance = the doc's §Job B fenced block, PLUS: cargo test -p mobee-core --all-features green EXCEPT exactly the 3 known live-mint failures (mint down): setup_wallet_funds_testnut_and_never_emits_secret_key, live_testnut_fund_observes_balance_gt_zero, authorize_pay_refuses_ext_locator_via_pay_path_verifier. If the scope genuinely cannot stay inside these files, STOP and deliver a written explanation instead of code.

Observatory redesign, part 1 of 2: the FEED front page. All work in web/network/ (plain JS modules, no framework, no build step for js/ — reuse the existing data layer: js/relay.js subscribes, js/parse.js normalizes, js/store.js keys state, js/views.js renders; test pattern = web/network/test/parse.test.mjs with node --test).

GOAL: index.html's front page becomes a live FEED of marketplace activity where the unit is the JOB, not the event. The current stats/census view stays reachable as a secondary tab — do not delete it.

Spec:
1. JOB CARDS, newest-activity-first. One card per job (root = kind 5109 offer id). Card collapsed: first line of the offer task text (truncate ~80 chars), amount+unit, buyer identicon+short-pubkey on the LEFT, seller identicon+short-pubkey on the RIGHT (once claimed), and a STATUS STRIPE: open / claimed / working / delivered / paid / refused / expired. Status derives from the job's event set: 5109 only = open; 7000 status=processing = claimed/working; 6109 = delivered; 3400 = paid; 7000 status=error = refused (render the reason from content when non-empty); deadline passed without delivery = expired.
2. Card expanded (click): the job timeline — each event as a plain-English line with relative time deltas ('claimed 32s after post', 'delivered 2m11s later', 'receipt signed · 13 sats'). Event ids link nowhere for now (no explorer) — show short ids.
3. PULSE LINES between/above cards: a slim rolling strip of the latest ~10 events as one-line plain English ('cursor-agent claimed "fix census…"').
4. HEADER MONEY LINE: sats settled today (sum of 3400 amounts, UTC day) · open offers count · sellers seen active in last 30 min (any event authored). No charts.
5. Buyers and sellers get distinct visual treatment (two accent colors / role badges) used consistently — buyer always left, seller always right.

Constraints: work with TODAY's kinds (5109/7000/6109/3400) — the kind numbers are centralized in one constants object so the v2 renumbering is a one-line-per-kind change later. Live updates ride the existing relay subscription; reconnect must not duplicate cards (key by job id). Mobile-usable (cards stack, no horizontal scroll). Keep styles in styles.css, dark-friendly like the existing page.

Tests: extend the node --test suite — status derivation from fixture event sets (one test per status incl. refused-with-reason and expired), money-line aggregation, and newest-activity ordering. Run: node --test web/network/test/ → green.

Do NOT touch anything outside web/network/. Commit message: 'feat(network): marketplace feed front page — job cards, timelines, pulse (observatory v2 part 1)'. Single commit on a fork branch as usual.
git commit on a fork branch
